notes: The verse's two doctrinally load-bearing terms are dhruvaḥ ('certain' / 'inevitable') and aparihārye 'rthe ('in the unavoidable matter'). Both are preserved in shape: the engineering layer renders dhruvaḥ as 'has a deprecation ahead of it... has a replacement ahead of that' (the certainty is both directions), and aparihārye 'rthe as 'in a matter that cannot be avoided.' The Prabhupada-interpretive rendering ('in the unavoidable discharge of your duty') is honestly noted as an interpretive insertion in the disagreement entry; the engineering layer follows the Shankara/Ramanuja literal reading that the verse stays on the metaphysical leg of the argument. No invented Sanskrit, no misattribution. PASS.
